... [5 lines omitted] ...

pytest failed: 21 issues found
### All Failing Tests
1. **tests/cli/commands/split/test_plan.py::test_execute_split_plan_creates_worktrees** — AttributeError: 'FakeGit' object has no attribute 'worktree'
2. **tests/cli/commands/split/test_plan.py::test_execute_split_plan_with_noop_ops** — AttributeError: 'MockDryRunGit' object has no attribute 'worktree'
3. **tests/commands/admin/test_plan_implement_workflow.py::test_creates_issue_when_not_provided** — Expected string "Created test plan #1" not found in output
4. **tests/commands/branch/test_checkout_cmd.py::test_checkout_for_plan_creates_impl_folder** — Expected "Created .erk/impl-context/ folder from plan #500" but got "...from PR #500"
5. **tests/commands/branch/test_checkout_cmd.py::test_checkout_for_plan_prints_activation_when_sync_status_fails** — Expected "...from plan #600" but got "...from PR #600"
... [54 lines omitted] ...
**Test Results:** 21 failed, 6744 passed, 2 warnings in 43.95s The primary pattern across these failures indicates that the codebase has been updated to use PR terminology instead of plan terminology in help strings and user-facing messages (hence the branch name `plnd/O9109-update-help-strings-03-10-0937`), but the tests have not been updated to match these changes. Additionally, there are 2 test failures related to missing `worktree` attributes in fake Git objects and one metadata schema test failure related to attribute naming (`pr_number` vs `issue_number`).

    assert "### Diagnosis" in body
    assert "Duplicate PR" in body"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_fetch_context.py","old_string":"    def test_plan_not_found(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """Returns error JSON when plan not found."""
        objective = _make_issue(number=6423, title="My Objective", body="objective body")
        pr = _make_pr_details(number=6517, title="PR Title", body="pr body")

        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={6423: objective})
        # No plan PR configured for the branch - will trigger PlanNotFound
        fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub(pr_details={6517: pr}, issues_gateway=fake_issues)
        planned_pr_backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime())

        runner = CliRunner()
        result = runner.invoke(
            objective_fetch_context,
            ["--pr", "6517", "--objective", "6423", "--branch", "plnd/some-branch-01-01-1200"],
            obj=context_for_test(
                github=fake_github,
                plan_store=planned_pr_backend,
                repo_root=tmp_path,
                cwd=tmp_path,
                repo_info=_TEST_REPO_INFO,
            ),
        )

        assert result.exit_code == 1
        data = json.loads(result.output)
        assert data["success"] is False
        assert "No plan found for branch" in data["error"]","new_string":"    def test_plan_not_found(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """Returns error JSON when plan not found."""
        objective = _make_issue(number=6423, title="My Objective", body="objective body")
        pr = _make_pr_details(number=6517, title="PR Title", body="pr body")

        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={6423: objective})
        # No plan PR configured for the branch - will trigger PlanNotFound
        fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub(pr_details={6517: pr}, issues_gateway=fake_issues)
        planned_pr_backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime())

        runner = CliRunner()
        result = runner.invoke(
            objective_fetch_context,
            ["--pr", "6517", "--objective", "6423", "--branch", "plnd/some-branch-01-01-1200"],
            obj=context_for_test(
                github=fake_github,
                plan_store=planned_pr_backend,
                repo_root=tmp_path,
                cwd=tmp_path,
                repo_info=_TEST_REPO_INFO,
            ),
        )

        assert result.exit_code == 1
        data = json.loads(result.output)
        assert data["success"] is False
        assert "No PR found for branch" in data["error"]"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_fetch_context.py","old_string":"    def test_planned_pr_plan_not_found(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """Returns error when no PR exists for a plan-... branch."""
        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
        fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub()
        planned_pr_backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime())

        runner = CliRunner()
        result = runner.invoke(
            objective_fetch_context,
            ["--pr", "8002", "--objective", "7419", "--branch", "plan-no-such-plan"],
            obj=context_for_test(
                github=fake_github,
                plan_store=planned_pr_backend,
                repo_root=tmp_path,
                cwd=tmp_path,
                repo_info=_TEST_REPO_INFO,
            ),
        )

        assert result.exit_code == 1
        data = json.loads(result.output)
        assert data["success"] is False
        assert "No plan found for branch" in data["error"]","new_string":"    def test_planned_pr_plan_not_found(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """Returns error when no PR exists for a plan-... branch."""
        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
        fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub()
        planned_pr_backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime())

        runner = CliRunner()
        result = runner.invoke(
            objective_fetch_context,
            ["--pr", "8002", "--objective", "7419", "--branch", "plan-no-such-plan"],
            obj=context_for_test(
                github=fake_github,
                plan_store=planned_pr_backend,
                repo_root=tmp_path,
                cwd=tmp_path,
                repo_info=_TEST_REPO_INFO,
            ),
        )

        assert result.exit_code == 1
        data = json.loads(result.output)
        assert data["success"] is False
        assert "No PR found for branch" in data["error"]"}
